footer.tagline: A Mantle publication starter with seeded editorial structure.

sections.title: {{BRAND}} is ready to publish its first useful notes
A clean publication shape before the archive gets large
sections.body: Use this starter when the first product is a readable stream of posts, changelogs, essays, notes, or documentation.

items.body: The public page starts with a small editorial promise and a few seeded pieces instead of an empty blog shell.
Mantle stores posts as structured content, so the owner can later add authors, tags, subscriptions, review flow, or member-only entries.

sections.title: Seeded pieces to replace after launch
sections.body: These cards mirror the starter posts in the manifest seed. Keep the shape, then rewrite the titles and excerpts.

items.title: Welcome to {{BRAND}}
items.body: A short opening note that explains what this publication will help readers understand.

items.title: How this publication will grow
items.body: A plain update that demonstrates the post model before richer editorial features exist.

items.title: Next dispatch draft
items.body: A draft placeholder for the first real note the owner should write with their coding agent.

sections.title: The starter ships an editorial model, not a theme
sections.body: Publication keeps the same blank Kiwa runtime as every other type. The type-specific work lives in the manifest and seed.

items.title: Posts are the core
items.body: The `posts` schema starts with slug, title, excerpt, body, and publishedAt.

items.title: A public view is ready
items.body: `published-posts` exposes published entries for a future index or detail route.

items.title: Suggestions are optional
items.body: The suggestion procedure is available when the owner wants a lightweight reader feedback path.

sections.title: Questions to answer before adding more publication code
sections.body: Use these as replacement prompts for the real editorial plan.

items.title: What should the first real post explain?
items.body: Start with one useful note for the intended audience before adding categories or complex templates.

items.title: Does this need authors, tags, or subscriptions now?
items.body: Not yet. Add them only when the owner has real content that needs those fields.

items.title: Where should the agent work next?
items.body: Read `.mantle/launch-state.json`, inspect `manifests/publication.yaml`, then replace this seed with real editorial content.

sections.title: Turn {{BRAND}} into a real publication
sections.body: Replace the seeded posts, decide whether the home page should become an archive, and push the first real content update.

posts.title: Welcome to {{BRAND}}
posts.body:
{{DESCRIPTION}}

This first note is intentionally short. Replace it with the first useful article, update, or field note for the audience.

posts.title: How this publication will grow
posts.excerpt: A starter update that shows the publication model before the archive gets complex.
posts.body: Mantle starts with a small posts schema and a public published-posts view. Add authors, tags, subscriptions, or gated posts only after the first real publishing workflow needs them.

posts.title: Next dispatch draft
posts.excerpt: A draft placeholder for the first real editorial update.
posts.body: Use this draft to outline the next article before changing the homepage layout.
